title: DSDS Document Block — Scoped Unions
description: Defines scoped document block unions, one per artifact type. Each piece of structured docs attached to an artifact is a document block object with a `kind` tag. Each artifact type accepts its own specialized block kinds plus every general kind (see `generalDocumentBlock`). General kinds — including the free-form `section` block — are available on every artifact; specialized kinds are scoped so irrelevant docs (such as an API spec on a foundation) cannot be attached. Express internal artifact links through the unified `links` array on each entity, not through a dedicated block kind.

generalDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind available on every artifact type. General blocks cover cross-cutting concerns that apply to any artifact, including free-form narrative prose. Every scoped union includes these kinds by referencing this definition.

oneOf.description: Actionable usage rules paired with rationale. Tells the reader what to do (or not do) and why.
Scenario-driven guidance for when to use and when not to use the artifact.
Structured accessibility specs: keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, screen reader behavior, focus management, color contrast, and motion considerations.
Structured content guidelines: recommended action labels with definitions, usage context, and cross-references, plus localization and i18n considerations.

Free-form narrative prose organized into titled, optionally nested sections — for conceptual content the structured block kinds do not capture: rationale, background, decision history, and FAQs. Reach for a structured block kind first; use `section` only for what they cannot express.

componentDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on component artifacts. Includes the component-specific kinds (import, anatomy, API, variants, states, design specs) plus every general kind.

oneOf.description: How to import the component across platforms — package names, import statements, and setup context.
The visual structure of the component: named sub-elements (parts), their design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.

The code-level interface: properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. For multi-platform components, include one API block per platform.
All dimensions of visual or behavioral variation. Each axis (size, emphasis, and so on) carries its values, token overrides, previews, and purpose. Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
All interactive states the component can enter (hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, and the like). Each state carries its triggers, token overrides, and previews.
Measurable visual specs: token inventory, spacing relationships, dimension constraints, typography settings, and responsive behavior.
Any general block kind: guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, or a free-form section.

foundationDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on foundation artifacts. Includes the foundation-specific kinds (principles, scales, motion) plus every general kind.

oneOf.description: High-level principles that shape decisions for the visual domain: what the system believes and which trade-offs it accepts.
An ordered sequence of values forming a visual scale: a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or similar progression. Each step references a design token.
Named easing curves with cubic-bezier functions (aligned with the DTCG cubicBezier type), recommended durations, and usage guidance for the motion system.
Any general block kind: guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, or a free-form section.

patternDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes the pattern-specific kind (interactions), shared structural kinds (anatomy, variants, states), and every general kind.

oneOf.description: The interaction flow of the pattern: an ordered sequence of steps describing triggers, system responses, and component behavior.
The visual structure of the pattern: named sub-elements (image, title, body, primary action, and so on), their design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.
Dimensions of variation within the pattern: sub-types, modes, or configurations (e.g., 'no-data', 'user-action', 'error-management'). Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
States the pattern can enter (populated, empty, loading, error, and so on). Each state carries its triggers, visual changes, and optional previews.
Any general block kind: guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, or a free-form section.

tokenDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on token and token group artifacts. Tokens carry no component-, foundation-, or pattern-specific docs — only the general kinds (guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, and free-form sections).

oneOf.description: Any general block kind: guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, or a free-form section.

guideDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on guide artifacts. Includes the guide-specific procedure kind (steps), the reused import kind (setup and install instructions), and every general kind — among them the free-form `section` block that forms the backbone of conceptual guides. Guides are reading-oriented, so their blocks favor prose and procedures over the measurable specs (api, anatomy, design specs) used by components.

oneOf.description: An ordered or unordered procedure: the sequence of steps a reader follows to install, build, contribute, or migrate.
How to install and import the system or a package across platforms — package names, import statements, and setup context. Useful in getting-started guides.
Any general block kind: guideline, purpose, accessibility, content, or a free-form section.
